title: Test port allocation has TOCTOU race: std TcpListener dropped before tokio rebinds
description: tests/integration.rs:31-48: a std::net::TcpListener binds 127.0.0.1:0 to get a free port, then is dropped immediately (line 33), then a tokio::net::TcpListener tries to re-bind the same port number (line 46). Between drop and re-bind another process can steal the port, causing the test to fail with 'address already in use'. More subtly, the two parallel test instances can steal each other's ports. Fix: pass the std TcpListener's file descriptor directly to tokio using TcpListener::from_std(), eliminating the TOCTOU window entirely.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
